_doc: Per-protocol config for the analysis. prod_prefixes/exclude_substrings filter gasReportTable + RQ5 cell contracts to real production deployables. pre_rewrite_ref = git HEAD when gasopt ran (baseline for RQ5 cells A/B). Filled in incrementally as each protocol is processed.

morpho-blue.notes: foundry.toml default is via_ir + optimizer_runs=999999, so the Phase-3 gas.json is a via-IR (B->D) comparison; RQ5 factorial re-measures under runs=200 with via_ir as the only factor.

v4-core.notes: solc 0.8.26, evm cancun, via_ir + optimizer_runs=44444444 (very slow). remappings.txt shipped by the repo (no prep commit). Submodules were uninitialized on disk; ran git submodule update --init --recursive as Phase-2 prep (no tracked change).

core.notes: Lido. Multi-version (0.4.24/0.6.11/0.6.12/0.8.9/0.8.25); default profile optimizer_runs=200, per-path via_ir compilation_restrictions for contracts/0.8.25/** and contracts/upgrade/**. Deps vendored via `yarn install` (node-modules linker); remappings.txt = `forge remappings` committed as pre_rewrite_ref. Required the FIXED gasopt build (commit 3534bf2): range-pragma resolution, version-gated require-error/unchecked, version-aware ASTWriter, AST-only parse, verify via-IR retry + pre-existing-error baseline. 50/128 files optimised, 3 skips (LidoTemplate/WithdrawalQueueBase verify-reverted, OmnibusBase version-intersection) — all safely handled.

aave-v3-origin.notes: Aave V3 Origin (self-contained, 0 fork sites). solc 0.8.27, optimizer_runs=200, no via_ir default -> Phase-3 gas.json IS Cell C (gasopt+standard), so A->C is the gasopt-alone comparison. RQ5 factorial re-measures all 4 cells at runs=200. Gas run RE-DONE after a crash mid repeat 1/3; deterministic, 0 skippedAfterRewriteOnly, overall -0.221% suite gas (995 tests). remappings.txt tracked at pre_rewrite_ref (prep commit 'complete remappings'). RQ5 RESUME: Cells A/C measured (standard, runs=200, 3 deterministic runs); Cells B/D via-IR NOT measurable (solc SIGKILL/OOM on whole-codebase via-IR compile at 16GiB, confirmed also with a single run -> compile-time OOM, not run-count). A->C is the gasopt-alone comparison. RE-ATTEMPTED on 31GiB machine (mfav), forge 1.7.1, gasopt 751da96: rewrite reproduced byte-identically (66/186, same 10 rules), cells A/C re-measured (3 runs each, standard/200). Cells B/D via-IR STILL OOM even at 31GiB (whole-codebase via-IR compile exhausts >30GiB; a memory watchdog killed solc twice at MemAvailable<1.2GiB to protect the host). Conclusion: aave via-IR factorial is not measurable on this hardware; RQ5 for aave = A->C only.

openzeppelin-contracts.notes: OpenZeppelin Contracts (library). LIBRARY-EMBEDDED: OZ contracts are inherited/imported, never deployed standalone, so the Foundry gas report attributes their deploy cost + runtime gas to the test/mock contracts that embed them; the strict production filter yields no standalone deployable. Effectiveness (RQ3/RQ5) is therefore reported at 'library-embedded' scope (the embedding harnesses), never conflated with a standalone production-deploy saving. solc 0.8.31, evm osaka, optimizer_runs=200, no via_ir default -> gas.json IS Cell C (gasopt+standard); A->C is the gasopt-alone comparison. Self-contained, 0 fork sites, 31 foundry test files. Baseline (b60a4b9) = fresh git init of pristine upstream (original per-protocol .git gone; deps committed as plain files). Fase-2 prep: `npm ci` required (2 account-abstraction test suites read EntryPoint predeploy bytecode from node_modules/hardhat-predeploy/bin/ via vm.readFileBinary; without it forge snapshot aborts the whole baseline). node_modules is gitignored and symlinked into the worktree by the harness. Run on forge 1.7.1 to match the earlier protocols.

core-v3.notes: Gearbox V3 (leverage/credit). solc 0.8.23, evm shanghai, optimizer_runs=1000, no via_ir default -> gas.json IS Cell C. Self-contained (libs=[lib] only, no node_modules), remappings.txt shipped, 0 fork sites, 32 foundry test files. Baseline (c16226f) = fresh git init of pristine upstream. forge 1.7.1.

seaport.notes: Seaport (OpenSea NFT marketplace). solc 0.8.24, evm cancun; REQUIRED gasopt fix 751da96 (evm_version was dropped into PathOptions -> tload/tstore skipped as 'not found'; see tool-bugs.md Bug 6). factorial measured at optimizer_runs=200 (project default 4,294,967,295 is infeasible). RQ2 rewrite (report.json) fully OK after the fix: 20/48 files, 7 rules. RQ3/RQ5 come from the factorial cells A (orig+std) and C (gasopt+std), both 3 runs; via-IR cells B/D fail to compile at runs=200 (stack-too-deep, like Gearbox) -> via-IR column N/A. The differential gas.json (forge snapshot) could NOT be produced: Seaport's Foundry suite loads its optimized/reference implementations via `vm.getCode`, which needs those separate build profiles pre-compiled (the `optimized` profile is via-IR + 4.29B runs, infeasible), so `forge snapshot`'s strict baseline aborts on the setUp that calls vm.getCode. forge test --gas-report (used by the factorial) tolerates that failing contract and still measures the rest -- but the ONLY contracts the runnable suite actually deploys are the test tokens/helpers (contracts/test/TestERC20/721/1155, lib/test helpers), NOT Seaport's real production contracts, which the suite loads via `vm.getCode` from the pre-built optimized/reference artifacts (infeasible via-IR+4.29B builds). So the strict production filter correctly finds 0 seaport deployables and seaport is absent from RQ3/RQ5: its production gas is NOT measurable on this hardware (documented limitation). Seaport still contributes RQ1 (ran end-to-end) + RQ2 (20/48 files, 7 rules) and exposed the evm_version bug (tool-bugs.md Bug 6). Project default optimizer_runs=4,294,967,295 (max uint32) is infeasible; libs=[node_modules, lib]; remappings.txt generated via forge remappings and committed into the baseline (c6223c3). 53 foundry test files (test/foundry), 0 fork sites. forge 1.7.1.
